literal: intense and continuous meditation; intensive form from ni- + √dhyai 'to meditate'
invariant_definition: The third stage of *śravaṇa-manana-nididhyāsana*: intensive sustained meditation on what has been heard and reflected on, removing *pratibandhaka*s (residual obstructions, primarily *deha-ātma-buddhi* — the body-self identification) so that the cognition becomes existentially operative. All Vedāntic schools accept *nididhyāsana*; differences in framing track those at *śravaṇa* and *manana*.

per_school.definition: *Nididhyāsana* removes *pratibandhakas* and stabilizes *brahma-jñāna* into *brahma-niṣṭhā*. In some Advaita texts (especially *Vivekacūḍāmaṇi* and *Pañcadaśī*) *nididhyāsana* is given a heavier weight than the bare *śravaṇa*-account suggests.
*Nididhyāsana* is essentially *upāsana* — the sustained meditational devotional stance that constitutes mature *bhakti*.
